own_review_points: containsAnyCreatedScopeKeys calls vertxContext.getLocal(REQUEST_SCOPED_LOCAL_KEY) — the PR's own VertxLocalsHelper shows ContextLocal keys need the AccessMode overload, so plain getLocal may not hit the optimized store and could always return null
duplicateContextIfContainsAnyCreatedScopeKeys is a misleading name — it does not always duplicate (human-ref)
Re-introduction of a special-case branch for @RequestScoped in create(); compareAndSet vs compareAndExchange question (human-ref)
removeLocal: if(localKey==null) is dead code — localKey comes from a value already proven non-null by instanceof
instanceof ContextLocalImpl<?> depends on a Vert.x internal impl class — fragile; assert fallback can be disabled (relates to human-ref :28)
ACCESS_TOGGLE_KEY becomes null when FULLY_DISABLED, but safety-toggle methods pass it to context.getLocal/putLocal
TODO acknowledges ContextLocal<?> entries are not copied on duplicate() — silent data loss for other extensions
requestScopedKeyCreated CAS flag is never reset on failure path
notes: Substantive, mostly grounded review of a tricky concurrency/Vert.x-API PR. Good catches: dead null-check in removeLocal, fragile instanceof on internal ContextLocalImpl (overlaps human :28), the getLocal dispatch concern (overlaps human :63), and the FULLY_DISABLED null-key risk. Trivial/praise: TODO restatement, getOrCreate javadoc-strengthening, requestScopedKeyCreated-not-reset (minor speculation), and the correct-double-checked-locking praise. Missed two human points: the misleading method name (:59) and the @RequestScoped special-casing + CAS-vs-exchange question (:42). net=2.
